abstract: A key whose value is the trust object the system uses to evaluate the validity of the code’s signature.
overview:
## Discussion

The value is a retained [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecTrust] object. You may use [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecTrust] functions (see [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/certificate-key-and-trust-services] to extract detailed information, for example the reasons why certificate validation may have failed. Because this object may continue to be used for further evaluations of the code signature, if you make any changes to it, the behavior of the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecTrust] functions is undefined.

Specify the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecCSSigningInformation] flag when calling the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecCodeCopySigningInformation(_:_:_:)] function to get this information.

# kSecCodeInfoTrust

**Global Variable**

A key whose value is the trust object the system uses to evaluate the validity of the code’s signature.

## Declaration

```swift
let kSecCodeInfoTrust: CFString
```

## Discussion

The value is a retained [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecTrust] object. You may use [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecTrust] functions (see [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/certificate-key-and-trust-services] to extract detailed information, for example the reasons why certificate validation may have failed. Because this object may continue to be used for further evaluations of the code signature, if you make any changes to it, the behavior of the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecTrust] functions is undefined.

Specify the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecCSSigningInformation] flag when calling the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecCodeCopySigningInformation(_:_:_:)] function to get this information.
title: kSecCodeInfoTrust
